summary: The actual screenshot shows a detailed gameplay dashboard with resources, army purchase, and hero assignment panels over a star map, while the wireframe is a simplistic placeholder with mismatched content and layout. Several visual and structural mismatches exist, and the wireframe is only partially suitable due to its lack of detail for precise validation.

correctElements.evidence: Both images feature a dark background with star systems, matching the global wireframe style requirements for dark starfield/game-map background.
Actual has a vertical panel on the left with resources and actions, similar to the wireframe's left panel, aligning with the screen description of a narrow command panel.
Both include circular orbits with labels like 'Sirius' and 'Canopus', consistent with gameplay map elements.
Top resource bars for POP, MET, FUEL are present in both, though details differ, satisfying basic resource display in the screen description.
A Map Key is present on the right in both, though content varies, matching the need for gameplay map visibility.

incorrectElements.expected: Wireframe shows three systems (Sirius, Arcturus, Canopus)
incorrectElements.whyIncorrect: Mismatch in map content; Arcturus is missing in actual, which may indicate incomplete implementation or different game state not captured in wireframe.
Text mismatch in turn phase description, potentially affecting user-facing behavior and not matching the documented screen description.

incorrectElements.expected: Placeholder 'Legacy Player...' with bounded scroll content, BACK and NEXT/APPLY buttons
incorrectElements.actual: Detailed sections: Resources with + increments, Army Purchase with Buy buttons, Hero Assignment with Assign buttons
incorrectElements.whyIncorrect: Wireframe uses placeholders while actual has specific functional content; this indicates the wireframe is too abstract for validating detailed UI behavior.

incorrectElements.expected: Narrow panel with thin green borders, fixed geometry at 1536x832
incorrectElements.actual: Panel is narrow but borders are thin green in wireframe; actual appears semi-transparent black with green highlights, slight bounds differences
incorrectElements.whyIncorrect: Minor bounds and styling mismatches, including DPI/scaling or z-order differences, affecting precise screenshot comparison.

incorrectElements.expected: No debug or panel toggle text
incorrectElements.actual: Text like 'Panels F1 Dbg:Off F2 Cmd:On' etc.
incorrectElements.whyIncorrect: Extra controls or debug info not present in wireframe, potentially violating clean user-facing UI behavior.

incorrectElements.actual: Detailed key with symbols for System orbit, Stellar body, etc.
incorrectElements.whyIncorrect: Content mismatch; wireframe lacks details present in actual, limiting validation of map elements.
Wireframe has baseline label, actual has different annotations, indicating residual debug artifacts.
Actual includes dynamic increments not represented in wireframe, a material mismatch for validating player resources.

incorrectElements.actual: Visual similarities but not exact due to rendering differences
incorrectElements.whyIncorrect: Even with close match, image-based comparison has limitations like compression artifacts or anti-aliasing, representing residual risk.

wireframeSuitability.why: The wireframe provides a basic layout baseline with correct resolution and style elements, but it uses too many placeholders and lacks detail on functional content, making it inadequate for fully validating detailed UI behavior and requirements like specific buttons or dynamic elements.
missingRequirements: Detailed representation of army purchase and hero assignment sections as per screen description
Dynamic elements like resource increments and territories/armies counts
Accurate map content including all expected star systems
recommendedWireframeChanges: Replace placeholders with specific labels, buttons, and sections matching the actual expected UI
Include dynamic placeholders for variable text like phase names and increments
Ensure all map elements (e.g., exact number of systems) match the baseline game state
Remove or standardize debug annotations for cleaner validation
